signal: Humanoids and mobile robots begin targeting repeatable physical work in factories, warehouses, inspection routes, and service environments.
Bodies, actuators, hands, batteries, sensors, and manufacturing scale define whether AI can become useful in the physical world.
Teleoperation, simulation, sensor logs, video, and robot fleet feedback become the training fuel for embodied intelligence.
Robots need charging, monitoring, fleet management, maintenance, safety tooling, mapping, and integration before they scale.
Most pure-play humanoid builders remain private, so public exposure comes through automation, semiconductors, industrial robotics, and parent companies.
Robots, autonomous machines, DePIN networks, and machine payments point toward a future where machines become economic actors.
